key_insights.insight: Relationship issues often stem from historical experiences, particularly those involving parental relationships, and aren't necessarily about the current partner.
key_insights.quote: All relationship issues are historical. It's not about them. It's about something that happened with mom or dad the things you're trying to change and fix were things that uh you needed from your parents

key_insights.insight: Enmeshment, a form of parenting where a child's role is to cater to the parent's emotional needs, can lead to feeling trapped in relationships and may manifest as a desire to escape, sometimes through cheating.
key_insights.quote: As soon as you're in a relationship againyou feel trapped and that trappedness reminds you of your parents your parents in your childhoodand so what do you want to do when you're trapped fly escape exactly you want to escape but how dowe escape cheating exactly interesting that cheating is a a path to escaping

key_insights.insight: Humility is essential for personal growth and change, acknowledging that you don't have all the answers and being open to expert guidance.
key_insights.quote: The first step to heal is humility. Like the number one thing you need is humility...you need really humility to say, shoot, I don't know the answers. And you just surrender to a, to a, to an expert and just say, I know nothing.

key_insights.insight: Healing involves a combination of deep emotional work, ongoing maintenance, and practical tools to address backsliding behaviors.
key_insights.quote: Deep intensive workshops, uh, ongoing maintenance throughgroup or talk therapy and tools to use when the old behaviors come up.

key_insights.insight: When triggered by a familiar stimulus that caused trauma in childhood, past protective mechanisms take over; it's important to disengage them by consciously recognizing that the present situation is safe.
key_insights.quote: When you see something that's familiarthat traumatized you as a child or a teen,right away your protective mechanisms from thenare going to take over.So you've got gotta like disengage itand so what works is saying it works is recognizing it and saying like no this isn't that this isactually okay so you can relax and just accept

key_insights.insight: To become more non-reactive in conflicts, one must learn to recognize their own early warning signs of stress and disengage before reacting.
key_insights.quote: What you want to do is start recognizing the signs of, oh, my heart's starting to beat faster...As soon as I feel that, I'll just say, one second, I'll be right back.I'll step back, bring myself back to earth,and then come back and I'll never react.

key_insights.insight: Cheating creates a divide and prevents authentic connection with your partner.
key_insights.quote: If you cheat on someone, the harm to you is all of a sudden you've taken the relationship and you just like drove a cleaver through it.And now you're living in a separate world than they are. You can't get back to the same intimacy that you had before.

key_insights.insight: When it comes to relationships, attract someone who is at your level of growth and self-esteem by focusing on becoming that ideal partner yourself.
key_insights.quote: You're going to attract someone who's at your level of growth and self-esteem...make that list for yourself and become that person.And then you'll meet that person.

key_insights.insight: Instead of focusing on authentic versus false self, consider whether your actions are creative (constructive) or destructive.
key_insights.quote: I realized that a better dichotomy is the creative versus the destructive self...was it constructive for you or was it destructive and so trying to do things that are constructive feels right

actionable_advice.advice: Disengage protective mechanisms by recognizing that current experiences are not like past traumas.
actionable_advice.how_to: When triggered, consciously tell yourself 'This isn't that. This is okay. You can relax and accept.'

actionable_advice.advice: Interrupt reactive behavior by recognizing your physical warning signs and taking a break.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify personal physical cues of stress (e.g., increased heart rate, tension in hands). When detected, excuse yourself, step back, and regain composure before reacting.

actionable_advice.advice: Train yourself even in small things to better yourself.
actionable_advice.how_to: Think of everything as an opportunity for practice and self-improvement, even your alone time.

actionable_advice.advice: Recognize and disengage from past programming.
actionable_advice.how_to: Run a virus scan of yourself to quarantine and change unwanted programs. Look back and see how these seeds were planted.
Ensure that you feel sexually, lovingly and communicatively full in your relationship.

memorable_stories: Neil's experience with sex addiction rehab and discovering emotional incest.
The story of the elderly man in a wheelchair surrounded by young women in a nightclub, serving as a cautionary tale about chasing ego validation.
Encountering someone after Hurricane Katrina who inspired the creation of his prepping book
contrarian_claims: It is natural for humans to be wired to cheat after about 7 years.
Checking your partner's mobile phone behind their back is a form of cheating.
In business relationships, its beneficial to disagree as a partnership requires different view points to prosper.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Former No.1 Pick-Up Artist: “We’re Wired to Cheat After 7 Years”, “I Was In A Relationship With My Mum”, The True Danger Of Porn: Neil Strauss
